goal: Relocate to Portland and be fully settled there in about three months.
constraints: proximity to transit or walkable neighborhoods is a must.
pet-friendly and safe for a small indoor cat.
decisions: Provide upfront cost estimates (deposits, movers, emergency buffer)
Develop short-term budget-friendly furniture plan with prioritized list and shopping budgets
Decide which plants to bring and packing/transport method (ventilation, moisture control, and regulatory compliance)
Evaluate furniture assembly options (Craigslist worker at $20/hr); obtain quotes and compare with other platforms (TaskRabbit/Handy)
Assess renter's insurance needs and determine coverage options (personal property, liability, ALE)
Evaluate national vs local moving options; cost-benefit analysis; gather quotes
Evaluate feasibility of mailing plants to destination as alternative to transporting plants; compare costs and regulations
entities: plant mailing policies and carrier options (USPS, FedEx, UPS)
mailing plants rules and packaging requirements
open: Target move date / settle date

Narrow this down to three prioritized neighborhoods based on exact commute assumptions
Draft a short message template to landlords asking about pet policy and cat-safety details

Decision: national vs local moving options - ongoing
Wardrobe packing plan: capsule wardrobe and outfit kit for moving day

Plant mailing feasibility: evaluate cost, rules, and logistics for mailing plants; decide which plants to mail vs bring

Day-one box plan: contents to include (kettle, mugs, change of clothes, chargers, basic toolkit)
